title: lamiastrum galeobdolon subsp. argentatum
intro: Lamiastrum galeobdolon subsp. argentatum is a lesser-known subspecies of the golden dead nettle plant, characterized by its silvery-green foliage. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health issues due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Lamiastrum galeobdolon subsp. argentatum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and soothe respiratory problems, making it a popular ingredient in herbal teas and infusions. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water to create a medicinal tea, or by infusing the plant in oil to create a topical salve.
